page: Vultr voc-g-24c-96gb-480s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, built on the x86_64 architecture. It features an AMD EPYC-Rome processor operating at 2.0 GHz, configured with 24 physical cores and 24 vCPUs, yielding 1.0 thread per core. The CPU allocation is shared. Memory capacity is 96.0 GB, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 480 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In synthetic benchmarks, the processor demonstrates strong performance characteristics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance benchmark registers at 3208.88, placing it in the top tier (top 10%). The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance reaches 74749.71, which is categorized as strong (top 25%). The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AES, SHA_NI, BMI2, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 extensions.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is its shared CPU allocation, which may introduce resource contention under sustained, simultaneous multi-tenant workloads. However, the high resource density of 24 cores and 96.0 GB of RAM, paired with fast local NVMe SSD storage, offers a cost-effective profile for general-purpose workloads that require substantial memory and parallel processing without requiring dedicated CPU threads. This server is suitable for multi-threaded applications, web serving, database hosting, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
description: The Vultr voc-g-24c-96gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with 24 physical cores and 24 vCPUs on a shared CPU allocation. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the system includes 96.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and 480 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 3208.88 (top 10%) and strong multi-core performance with a score of 74749.71 (top 25%). Supported instruction sets include AVX, AES, and SHA_NI. While the shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for sustained peak workloads, the high core count, balanced memory ratio, and fast local storage make this instance a cost-effective option for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-24c-96gb-480s-amd is a shared-CPU instance featuring 24 AMD EPYC-Rome cores, 96 GB RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-24c-96gb-480s-amd features 24 AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB RAM, and 480 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong multi-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A balanced 24-core AMD EPYC instance with 96 GB RAM and local NVMe storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
